title: eshkol-fmt + eshkol-doc + (help foo) — developer tooling triad
goal: Three small developer-tooling features that together raise the day-to-day pleasure of using Eshkol substantially: eshkol-fmt (formatter), eshkol-doc (Markdown reference from `(provide …)` graph + docstrings), and (help foo) REPL reflection. The substrate already exists — procedure-arity, describe, the parser walker — these tasks bind it into user-facing commands.
next_actions: eshkol-fmt: read an .esk file, parse with the existing parser, reprint via a canonical indent/spacing walker. Idempotent: fmt twice == fmt once. Preserves comments and string interpolation segments. CLI flags: --check, --write, --stdout.

eshkol-doc: walk the module graph reachable from a given root .esk file. For each (provide …) target, emit a Markdown section with the procedure's signature (via procedure-arity), docstring (parsed from leading `;;@` line block immediately above the define), and any (define-test …) examples.
(help foo): REPL command that calls (describe foo) + (procedure-arity foo) + a docstring lookup. Renders in the REPL with ANSI color when isatty(stdout).
CMakeLists.txt: add executable targets eshkol-fmt and eshkol-doc; install alongside eshkol-run.

tests: eshkol_fmt_test.sh round-trips 5 representative files; eshkol_doc_test.sh verifies generated Markdown contains every export of core.list; repl_help_test.esk asserts (help map) produces a non-empty string containing 'arity'.
acceptance: eshkol-fmt foo.esk > foo.fmt.esk; eshkol-fmt foo.fmt.esk == foo.fmt.esk (idempotent).
eshkol-doc lib/stdlib.esk produces a Markdown file referencing every stdlib export.
(help map) inside a REPL session prints the arity, parameter names, and the docstring.
ux_payoff: Three of the most-asked-for ergonomic features. eshkol-fmt joins the modern-language tooling baseline; eshkol-doc makes the stdlib browsable; (help foo) makes the REPL self-documenting.
